data.content:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so MSBuild doesn't know to compile it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `@(Compile)` within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk, but MSBuild's compilation step doesn't know about it because it wasn't included in the `@(Compile)` items.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` within the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items after it's been generated. Specifically, I need to add:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

after the `WriteCodeFile` task call.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The generated file needs to be added to the `@(Compile)` item group so Roslyn picks it up.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after the file is written.
